posts.text: I recently visited Hiroshima, and stood at the epicenter of a city scarred by the unimaginable horror caused by a single nuclear bomb dropped in 1945. What I saw, the  stories I heard, and the haunting sadness that remains, will stay with me forever.

posts.text: Thank you, President Trump, for the opportunity to serve as a member of your cabinet to defend the safety, security and freedom of the American people.  I look forward to getting to work.

posts.text: The best way for us to honor our veterans is to only send our men and women in uniform into harm's way as a last resort, when no other options exist, and actually take care of them and their families if and when they return home. As a Lieutenant Colonel in the Army Reserves, serving as a solider for over 21 years with countless great Americas, this is the actual way to honor our veterans. #VeteransDay
